text.title: Deliverability Incident Runbook Worked Example
text.summary: Worked example: spam complaint spike on welcome sequence segment B after a subject-line A/B variant rollout.

section.title: Scenario
section.p: Tuesday 08:30: a subject-line variant rolls out for welcome_seq_segment_b. At 09:42 the post-send analytics show 0.18% complaint rate (threshold 0.1%). Bounce healthy at 2.4%; unsub at 1.6%.

section.title: Run
section.code: 1. Detect: complaint 0.18 > 0.1 threshold ✓ → incident 2. Isolate: segment_b vs segment_a → complaints concentrated in segment_b only Template "welcome_v3" + subject variant "B" implicated 3. Diagnose: - DKIM lookup: pass (mxtoolbox URL pinned) - ESP reputation: 82 (was 91) — 9pp drop - Complaint sample (5 of 6): all from gmail.com; subject contains "WIN" caps 4. Contain: pause-segment welcome_seq_segment_b auto_resume_at = next day 09:00 warmup_curve = 10/30/100 over 3 sends Replace subject variant B with control C; update suppression list with the 6 complainants only 5. Postmortem: published to incident log with all sources

list.item: Complaint rate dropped to 0.04% on next send post-warm-up.
ESP reputation recovered to 88 over 5 days.
Subject-line lint rule added: reject all-caps tokens like "WIN", "FREE".
